Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sally Liu, 415-635-1783 Plaintiff in Pro Per via Court Call.
Tom Normandin, Esq. 714-547-2444 x133 of Prenovost Normandin Bergh & Dawe for Defendants via Zoom.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court calls Lines #5 - 588317; Line #6 -588528 and Line #7 - 589085 simultaneously. Defense requests to notice a deposition to 9/27/21 instead of 9/23/21 that is indicated on the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues all Demurrers. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS LORI GETZ, DANIEL ROMAN, BRIAN GETZ AND LC EQUITY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

The Court adopts the tentative ruling on all Demurrers. Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege any viable cause of action against the defendants. For each cause of action Plaintiff must allege facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of such cause of action. No cause of action may arise out of defendants' actions prior to loan origination (e.g. misrepresenting as to the proposed terms of the loan) or based on the matters appearing on the face of the Deed of Trust or Note (e.g. no causes of action arising out of unlicensed lending by individuals listed in Attachment A to the Deed of Trust recorded on July 15, 2016), as such claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff may allege in good faith causes of action arising out of breach of the terms of the Note or Deed of Trust, if such breaches occurred within 4 years of filing of the complaint; or causes of action based on violations of relevant statutes (e.g. foreclosure statutes regarding). If Plaintiff choses to make such allegations, a copy of the Note shall be attached to the amended complaint. If Plaintiff proceeds with her claim for quiet title (as long as it is based on the grounds that are not time-barred), Plaintiff must allege tender the original amount of the loan (as stated in the Deed of Trust) that is not in dispute as having been received by Plaintiff. The Court does not express any opinion at this time as to propriety or viability of any causes of action that Plaintiff may be alleging. Any references to specific allegation that may be made (in good faith only) are made only as an example.

All causes of action shall be stated in clear and concise language. While not required, Plaintiff is encouraged to include relevant factual allegations within each cause of action. Such formatting of the operative complaint will ensure that all elements of each cause of action are properly alleged. Plaintiff shall have 60 days from notice of entry of order to file her amended complaint. Defendant to prepare an order in accordance with CRC 3.1312(a) and (b). If defendant fails to prepare an order as stated above, Plaintiff to prepare an order in accordance with CRC 3.1312(d).

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Proposed order to be dropped in Dept. 501's inbox.
